prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for benzaldehyde were computed at the DFT level using B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier 'standard') in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0 / multiplicity 1 for the neutral species (cation: charge +1, mult 2; anion: charge −1, mult 2). Partial charges were obtained via Mulliken population analysis and Fukui indices derived by finite difference. The geometry was taken directly from the user-supplied .xyz file (not re-optimized at the DFT level).

Headline result: the most electrophilic site (highest f⁺) is atom C-3 (ring carbon, 1-based index 3) with f⁺ = 0.39938963. The most nucleophilic site (highest f⁻) is atom O-1 (aldehyde oxygen) with f⁻ = 0.23839906. The dual descriptor (f⁺ − f⁻) is largest at C-3 (0.36263551), confirming its dominant electrophilic character.

All integrity checks passed (trustworthy: true): sum(f⁺) = 1.0000 and sum(f⁻) = 1.0000. No engine warnings were reported.
